preamble: The shift from [h] to [ʔ] involves the loss of continuous friction in favor of a complete glottal closure. It often serves as an intermediate stage between aspiration and total elision.

examples.note: Glottal reinforcement replacing initial aspiration in specific rhythmic contexts.

sources: Ladefoged, P. & Johnson, K. (2015). A Course in Phonetics (7th ed.). Cengage Learning.
